payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: As you should. Infecting friends is fun. Couple other things. For those who haven't seen it already, the D&Diesel videos went up earlier this week, where we had the wonderful chance-- for me, at least-- to Dungeon Master for a half an hour for Vin Diesel, along with Travis and Laura, and Jessica and Dan of Nerdist ilk. Had a great time, so hopefully you liked it, and if you'd like to see more stuff like that, hopefully we'll have more things coming up in the future in that vein, because we had a good time. Also, along with that, I released my Witch Hunter class, which was a customized 5th edition class that I designed after I ran that game, based on the character I created for Vin. It went up on the website, untested. Feedback is always welcome, and I will be updating it on the website as feedback comes in and balancing is tweaked for it. But have at it, have fun, hope you guys enjoy it. I had a good time creating it, and it's a good time. Also, just a reminder to you guys: our official Critical Role t-shirts are available on the Geek and Sundry website store if you want to wear any of the awesome red shirts that we've shown on the show with our full-on Critical Role logo. Shown in an exemplary dark, fine ink. Those are available for purchase.

preceding_turns.text: They're mad, you're allowed.

preceding_turns.text: I'm going to show you some music.

preceding_turns.text: It's good. Go download Spotify right now, so you can go, if you haven't. It's free!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: There you go.

preceding_turns.text: It's cancer. It's been a weird weeknight.

preceding_turns.text: It has. It's been a long day.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Well played. So. To give us a little overview of the current story. Two nasty individuals known as the Briarwoods, Lord and Lady Briarwood, have, for the past five years, been occupying the city of Whitestone, which was once the home to Percival here, after it was forcefully taken from his family, most of which seemingly have been either killed or lost in the process, and he was scattered to the four winds to find Vox Machina. After encountering the Briarwoods in Emon in a scuffle that took place, the Briarwoods retreated to Whitestone, inviting the party to come and say hello. Since then, the party has prepared the best they could, made their way towards Whitestone, and, through some bit of infiltration and subterfuge, managed to sneak into the city itself. They, under the guise of Scanlan's Seeming illusion, appeared to be as common folk as the rest of the poor saps that still live in Whitestone. They scoped out an abandoned tavern, which they fitted to their needs as a base of operations, and also managed to come across a central portion of Whitestone's city structure and history, known as the Sun Tree, a tree once risen to the might of the god of the sun, Pelor, now lies dead in the center of town, and from it hung eight figures that appear to have been individuals killed, hung, and painted up and dressed to resemble Vox Machina as essentially a warning, or a welcome. It's hard to tell when it comes to the Briarwoods. Nevertheless, a message was definitely delivered. The party, retreating back to the tavern, set up for the remainder of the afternoon. Keyleth carved, through her druidic magical ways, through the ground to find some of the roots of the Sun Tree, and began preparing an extensive, time-consuming ritual in hopes of restoring some semblance of life to the entity itself. That is where we left off. While the rest of you have been going about your preparations for the rest of the afternoon as this ritual for Keyleth is an eight-hour ritual and it begun in the early afternoon around 2:00, so this will conclude by around sundown, a little past sundown. While she is down there, essentially meditating with the barren roots of the Sun Tree, is there anything you guys would wish to do, or are you just waiting for her to complete her ritual?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh! Mazel tov!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, glad I came here instead of going to the award show. Totally worth it. But we won a Geekie. So, I thought I should say that.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Well-earned.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Thanks.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You nerd.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Good on you.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You should wear it like bling. Get a chain for it. That'd be great.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Just, once again, like, it-- No.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: No. No, never mind.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All righty, any other announcements anyone needs to make?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: God damn it, it keeps happening.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm good.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All righty. Well then, let us go ahead and get ourselves tuned in for tonight's episode of Critical Role. [dramatic music]

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And welcome back. So, everyone, our Sam arrived--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: We have our samurai! Laura and Travis are in transit, they'll be here as soon as they-- (thump from Sam's mug)

preceding_turns.text: (grunts in agreement)

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: In three, two.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (gasping for breath) "Please don't kill me. I can show you a secret entrance into the castle. I know a way in."

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It's not guarded?

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: "It's right--" He spits and puts a little droplet. It's loosely in the area of the western side of the mountain that the castle is built on. Roughly around the entrance where you escaped when you and Cassandra left Whitestone, five years before.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You've no idea what they want?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Comparatively, I'm just a peasant to them. I work with-- well, I worked with Kerrion."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: How long have you worked with Sir Stonefell?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Five years."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Were you here when he took the castle?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Yes, yes. I was part of the incursion."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Good. You're very lucky. Because you're going to live if you show us-- And I bring out a map.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: He can't see anything.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I know. Drop the Blindness.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He hears the crinkling of paper.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Drop the Blindness.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right. I drop Blindness and I drop Seeming.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (scared, fast breathing)

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Look at me. Look at me. You're going to show me where on the castle is the secret entrance. Do you know who I am?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "No."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You will. Mark it on the map.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He looks down at his bound arms and legs.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I take his head and I shove his nose and his face right above the map. And I say: use your nose.

payload.speaker: MATT
payload.raw_text: Okay. It will take you a few minutes. You now see, as you're finishing the symbol, Vouk running to the front of the building, and you hear the voices of two guards going, "Master Vouk? What happened? Tell us. Unbind him!" "Yes, sir." And the two guards at the front of the building are talking with him. You finish the symbol.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Give me your symbol.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I give her one of the parchments.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You fly up. The rain is coming down on you. It's a little hard to fly. You get up to the top of the roof.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I cast Flaming Sphere.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You transform into yourself again?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All righty.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We're all going to start going out the way we came.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I throw unconscious guy and Sir Dead.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Hope you guys were ready to get out.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I open up a bottle of wine and take a giant swig.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. As you guys head back down to the cellar?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You guys all head back down to the cellar. You throw them both out the window, including the body of Sir Kerrion (boof) out into the yard. You head back down into the cellar.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I cast Flaming Sphere, and I start moving it around on the roof and start drawing the shape of the de Rolo family crest on the roof.
